priorities: Capital preservation and wealth protection for future generations
Steady, predictable cash flow from global dividend-paying companies
Maintenance of a defensive, blue‑chip core portfolio with high‑grade sovereign bonds
redLines: No exposure to high‑beta or speculative asset classes (e.g., US software, AI, mega‑cap tech)
Do not shift away from the existing defensive value allocation
Absolute block on any growth‑oriented, volatile equities
preferredSectors: Consumer staples (e.g., Nestlé, Procter & Gamble, Walmart, Costco)

investmentBehavior.timeHorizon: Long‑term, multi‑generational wealth preservation for grandchildren
investmentBehavior.liquidity: Low immediate liquidity demand; occasional cash withdrawals for family projects while keeping core equity intact
investmentBehavior.mandate: Conservative Defensive Global Value & Income Mandate

lifeEvents.description: CHF 150,000 withdrawn from cash accounts for a structural renovation of the family holiday home in Flims.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Liquidity need satisfied via cash; core equity positions left untouched.

lifeEvents.description: Discussion on preserving wealth for grandchildren; client requested a strict flag to block high‑beta speculative assets.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Reinforced defensive bias; explicit exclusion of speculative asset classes added to mandate.

lifeEvents.description: Opened a minor savings account for the oldest grandson, emphasizing traditional saving virtues and avoidance of trading apps.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Further emphasis on low‑risk, savings‑oriented products for the next generation.

lifeEvents.description: Thomas Keller announced early retirement; account handover protocol for the global defensive allocation was initiated.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Continuity of the defensive strategy ensured; no change to asset mix.

lifeEvents.description: CIO issued a Tactical Asset Allocation update recommending reduction in defensive value to fund mega‑cap US technology.
lifeEvents.portfolioImpact: Client likely to reject the recommendation due to explicit red lines against speculative tech.

personalProfile.interests: traditional saving virtues, mountain retreats, Swiss heritage
personalProfile.quirks: formal and reserved; prefers quiet, predictable investments; quick decision on dividend reinvestments
personalProfile.rapportTriggers: mention of Swiss consumer staples, family foundation, industrial hardware firms like Siemens
personalProfile.avoidTopics: speculative technology, competitor banks, aggressive trading strategies

keyQuotes: "I want to sleep at night, not speculate on Silicon Valley cloud bubbles."
"These abstract software and AI firms are valued on pure hype. It feels like the dot‑com crash of 2000 all over again."
"We expect the same quiet, global defensive value approach that Thomas Keller provided. No aggressive strategy shifts."
